pages.full_text:
Reform Act, so he served local people. His grand daughter left the Abbey to the N.T. in 1944.
    We walked around Lacock, very pretty, weavers' houses. Church closed.
    We had lunch in Lacock carpark, & read afterwards.
    __Bradford__ also has beautiful stonehouses, 2 churches, one a lovely Saxon chapel. A delicious cream tea at the Bridge Tea Room. Violin with Lion head.
    On towards Bath, we are spending the night at __Stowford Farm,__ west of Trowbridge. Originally a mill, again lots of water, very, very pretty. Cows, goats, hens, peacocks. Clean loos.
pages.notes: Text continues from previous page (mid-sentence 'Reform Act'). 'Bradford' almost certainly refers to Bradford-on-Avon, Wiltshire, given the context of Lacock and Trowbridge nearby. Small decorative mark (lip/kiss symbol) before 'Saxon chapel'. '****' annotation appears after 'Stowford Farm' underline, possibly a rating or cross-reference. 'N.T.' = National Trust.

pages.full_text:
__Bath__ Peter map read. We've brought Thomas a plaster angel with guitar. Seen 8 dining room chairs.
    We visited the Roman Baths with
pages.notes: Upper portion of page is occupied by several pencil sketches: a tall architectural column or facade detail (top-left), a hexagonal/octagonal tile or window-pane pattern (top-right), a loose curved/flowing pattern with arrows (middle-right, possibly a weaving or masonry pattern), and a semi-circular arch/scallop pattern (bottom-centre). These appear to be sketches of architectural details observed during the visit, likely from Lacock or Bradford-on-Avon churches or Bath. Text cuts off at end of page ('with') — continues on next spread. The word 'Bath' at start of text appears underlined as a heading.
